title: stachys recta subsp. grandiflora
intro: Stachys recta subsp. grandiflora, also known as large-flowered woundwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat wounds, burns, and skin infections. The medicinal properties of large-flowered woundwort have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and fighting against bacterial infections. Large-flowered woundwort can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
